channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Judge says he will order government to preserve Signal messages about Houthi military strike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday said he will order the Trump administration to preserve records of a text message chat in which senior national security officials discussed sensitive details of plans for a U.S. military strike against Yemen’s Houthis. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EPA offers industrial polluters a way to avoid rules on mercury, arsenic and other toxic chemicals
item.description: As part of a push to roll back dozens of environmental regulations, the Trump administration is offering coal-fired power plants and other industrial polluters a chance to receive exemptions from rules requiring them to reduce emissions of toxic chemicals, including mercury, arsenic and benzene.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump withdraws Rep. Elise Stefanik's nomination for UN ambassador, citing tight GOP House margin
item.description: The White House has pulled Rep. Elise Stefanik’s nomination to be U.S. ambassador to the United Nations.
item.dc:creator: Farnoush Amiri And Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Marine Corps bets on electric seagliders 'invisible' to enemy radar for medevac missions at sea
item.description: Regent scored another $10 million contract with the Marine Corps Warfighting Lab for its all-electric seaglider plane on Wednesday as U.S. defense policy shifts focus to oceanic theaters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House withdrawing Stefanik nomination to serve as US ambassador to UN
item.description: The White House has informed the chair of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee that it is withdrawing Rep. Elise Stefanik’s (R-N.Y.) nomination to serve as U.S. ambassador to the United Nations, reflecting how critical her vote is in the House to passing President Trump’s agenda.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump asks Stefanik to withdraw from UN ambassador contention over razor-thin House majority
item.description: Rep. Elise Stefanik's, R-N.Y., nomination to be UN ambassador was put in jeopardy despite bipartisan support.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dems have long history of supporting encrypted Signal app ahead of Trump chat leak
item.description: Democrats and liberal groups have a long history of touting the use of Signal before the Trump administration's Signal chat leak with the editor-in-chief of the Atlantic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top MS-13 leader arrested in Virginia: White House officials
item.description: Authorities have not yet released the arrestee's name, but Attorney General Pam Bondi referred to him as one of MS-13’s “top leaders.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University detained, video shows masked people handcuffing her
item.description: A lawyer for a Turkish national and doctoral student at Tufts University says she has been detained by Department of Homeland Security agents without explanation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: US cities located in states won by Trump would be most hurt by Canadian tariffs, an analysis finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. cities most vulnerable to a trade war with Canada turn out to largely be in the states that helped return Donald Trump to the White House — a sign of the possible political risk he's taking with his tariff plans.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump places 25% tariff on imported autos, expecting to raise $100 billion in tax revenues
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the tariffs would be detailed at a 4 p.m. EST news conference.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Florida considers bill to roll back child labor laws to fill jobs once held by undocumented migrants
item.description: Florida lawmakers are considering major changes to state child labor laws to address a potential shortage of migrant workers due to GOP-led immigration crackdowns, which would allow teens to work longer hours and earn lower wages.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge to hear arguments against pro-Palestinian activist Mahmoud Khalil
item.description: During Thursday morning's hearing, the federal government is expected to expand on its newest accusations against Mahmoud Khalil.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Is the fight over? Florida bill to allow guns on campus gets shot down
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) — A rare rejection happened at the state capitol as a Republican-led bill was voted down by a fellow GOP lawmaker. Senate Bill 814 would have allowed concealed carry on college campuses, but it died in its first committee stop.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Justice Department goes to bat for Trump in hush money case
item.description: The Justice Department went to bat for President Trump in his hush money criminal case Wednesday, urging that his prosecution be moved out of New York state court so he can attempt to toss his guilty verdict on presidential immunity grounds.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s new executive order could upend voting
item.description: The order tests the power of Trump’s authority and would require voters using a federal form to show proof of citizenship to register to vote in federal elections. It’s sure to be tested in court.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block, MPR News

item.title: DHS suspends green card processing for refugees, asylees
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) said it is pausing review of green card applications for some refugees and asylees, leaving in limbo those who came to the U.S. after fleeing unrest. The directive from U.S.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: The inside story of how a journalist was sent White House war plans
item.description: Jeffrey Goldberg, the editor of The Atlantic, said he was mistakenly added to a group chat with U.S. national security leaders about imminent military strikes on Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Erika Ryan, Ailsa Chang, Christopher Intagliata, and Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Why Israel's having some of its biggest protests since the war in Gaza began
item.description: They're demanding a deal between Israel and Hamas to release all the remaining hostages, and also demonstrating against government attempts to weaken the judiciary.
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf and Daniel Estrin, MPR News

item.title: Most Florida voters approve of President Trump, Gov. DeSantis in office, new poll shows
item.description: A new poll shows that the majority of Florida voters approve of the work being done by President Donald Trump and Gov. Ron DeSantis.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Another poll puts NYC mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani in 2nd place
item.description: Editor's note: The video above originally aired on Feb. 28. NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- With the New York City mayor's race heating up, Assemblymember Zohran Mamdani is polling ahead of Mayor Eric Adams, according to a poll by Honan Strategy Group.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11
